metadata.isolationBoundary: Default compatibility containment and prototype boundary for Strategy execution; not a final sandbox for hostile Strategy code.

tradeoffs.adapterMetadataImplications: Remains the default counted JS/TS adapter and local-dev fallback.
containmentGaps: Shares host process address space class with Node worker isolation.
Filesystem and network remain host/inherited in registry metadata.
tradeoffs.deterministicExecutionRisk: Harness blocks common nondeterministic APIs, but this is not a security boundary.
tradeoffs.developerErgonomics: Always available in local Node development.
tradeoffs.noPromotionDecision: Evaluation-only in v1.8; not promoted to production hostile-code isolation or new counted-play eligibility.
tradeoffs.resourceLimitNotes: Uses worker timeout, V8 resourceLimits, and output byte caps.
unresolvedProductionRisks: Not acceptable as final public hostile Strategy isolation.

metadata.isolationBoundary: Opt-in process boundary for Strategy execution with one-shot JSON IPC.

tradeoffs.adapterMetadataImplications: Prototype adapter remains opt-in and should not become default.
containmentGaps: Process boundary only; host filesystem and network policy rely on harness-level blocks.
tradeoffs.deterministicExecutionRisk: Same JS harness restrictions as worker-thread, with stronger process failure taxonomy.
tradeoffs.developerErgonomics: Runs anywhere Node subprocess spawning works.
tradeoffs.noPromotionDecision: Evaluation-only in v1.8; not promoted to production hostile-code isolation or new counted-play eligibility.
tradeoffs.resourceLimitNotes: Uses spawn timeout plus stdout/stderr byte caps.
unresolvedProductionRisks: Needs OS/container sandboxing before hostile public use.

metadata.isolationBoundary: Production-candidate Strategy execution boundary using a short-lived container plus strict JSON IPC.

tradeoffs.adapterMetadataImplications: Production-candidate label stays candidate-only in v1.8.
containmentGaps: Depends on Docker daemon, runtime configuration, image provenance, and host kernel controls.
tradeoffs.deterministicExecutionRisk: Reuses the JS harness and adds network/filesystem/container controls.
tradeoffs.developerErgonomics: Repeatable when Docker and node image are available; otherwise skipped.
tradeoffs.noPromotionDecision: Evaluation-only in v1.8; not promoted to production hostile-code isolation or new counted-play eligibility.
tradeoffs.resourceLimitNotes: Docker args request no network, read-only root, tmpfs, memory, CPU, PID cap, dropped capabilities, and no-new-privileges.
unresolvedProductionRisks: Needs image pinning, deployment hardening, abuse review, and kernel/runtime validation.

tradeoffs.adapterMetadataImplications: Registry remains disabled for normal play and counted results.
containmentGaps: Non-JS product/package semantics are not defined and Python remains experimental.
tradeoffs.deterministicExecutionRisk: Requires separate Python capability and package policy hardening.
tradeoffs.noPromotionDecision: Evaluation-only in v1.8; not promoted to production hostile-code isolation or new counted-play eligibility.
tradeoffs.resourceLimitNotes: Existing adapter has timeout and empty env but no production sandbox certification.
unresolvedProductionRisks: Needs Phase 54 semantics and future sandbox proof before any promotion.

tradeoffs.adapterMetadataImplications: Would require new registry id and product semantics.
containmentGaps: No repo adapter or local binary; official docs still recommend layered OS or VM isolation for untrusted code.
tradeoffs.deterministicExecutionRisk: Permissions help deny host APIs but do not replace deterministic runtime design.
tradeoffs.developerErgonomics: Would add a new runtime dependency and adapter.
tradeoffs.noPromotionDecision: Evaluation-only in v1.8; not promoted to production hostile-code isolation or new counted-play eligibility.
unresolvedProductionRisks: Permission bypass and dependency policy review.

containmentGaps: No adapter/toolchain; host must define every capability.
tradeoffs.deterministicExecutionRisk: Can improve capability shape but needs fuel/epoch/time/memory policy.
tradeoffs.noPromotionDecision: Evaluation-only in v1.8; not promoted to production hostile-code isolation or new counted-play eligibility.
unresolvedProductionRisks: Language support, package, and determinism gaps.

tradeoffs.adapterMetadataImplications: Could harden container-subprocess in a future milestone.
containmentGaps: No local runsc; requires container runtime integration.
tradeoffs.deterministicExecutionRisk: Improves syscall isolation but does not define Strategy determinism.
tradeoffs.developerErgonomics: Operational dependency beyond local repo scripts.
tradeoffs.noPromotionDecision: Evaluation-only in v1.8; not promoted to production hostile-code isolation or new counted-play eligibility.
tradeoffs.resourceLimitNotes: Would inherit container resource policy plus runsc overhead.

containmentGaps: No local microVM toolchain; high operations setup.
tradeoffs.deterministicExecutionRisk: Strong isolation does not automatically solve deterministic execution.
tradeoffs.developerErgonomics: Too heavy for default local v1.8 workflow.
tradeoffs.noPromotionDecision: Evaluation-only in v1.8; not promoted to production hostile-code isolation or new counted-play eligibility.
unresolvedProductionRisks: Fleet operations, cold start, and image security.

criteria.currentEvidence: The container candidate is optional and may be skipped in the committed artifact.
criteria.promotionGate: Skipped container probes block promotion; required checks must fail instead of falling back.
criteria.requirement: Run the full hostile probe matrix against the container subprocess candidate in a required lane.

criteria.currentEvidence: Adapter metadata declares requested Docker limits and hostile probes cover logical limits.
criteria.promotionGate: Production promotion requires live resource-limit evidence under normal and adversarial load.
criteria.requirement: Prove wall timeout, CPU, memory, PID, output, source, private memory, and private objective limits.

criteria.currentEvidence: Container adapter requests read-only root and tmpfs scratch; live proof is optional.
criteria.promotionGate: Filesystem denial must be live-proven before counted runtime promotion.
criteria.requirement: Prove read-only root, constrained scratch space, no host path access, and safe write-attempt classification.

criteria.currentEvidence: Container adapter requests Docker network none; live proof is optional.
criteria.promotionGate: Network-denial probes must pass in a required lane before promotion.
criteria.requirement: Prove no outbound network, DNS, localhost, metadata IP, proxy, or inherited token access.

criteria.currentEvidence: Default image is a mutable Node image reference.
criteria.promotionGate: Mutable or unreviewed images block production counted execution.
criteria.requirement: Pin or otherwise control image provenance, update policy, and vulnerability review.

criteria.currentEvidence: Current topology is local diagnostics and current counted execution remains worker-owned.
criteria.promotionGate: Promotion requires explicit deployment ownership and rollback semantics.
criteria.requirement: Define runtime process ownership, preflight, health/readiness, concurrency, rollback, and no web/API execution path.

criteria.currentEvidence: Runtime violation and system failure taxonomy exists for current adapters.
criteria.promotionGate: Ambiguous runtime failures block counted promotion.
criteria.requirement: Classify Strategy violations separately from adapter, daemon, image, cgroup, OOM, IPC, and crash failures.

criteria.currentEvidence: Sandbox artifacts are public-safe and topology diagnostics are redacted.
criteria.promotionGate: Any public diagnostic leak blocks promotion.
criteria.requirement: Keep public and monitor diagnostics free of Strategy source, memory, objectives, streams, stack traces, tokens, sessions, host paths, and private runtime internals.

criteria.currentEvidence: Default evaluation can skip Docker; required container commands are explicit.
criteria.promotionGate: Silent fallback from container to worker, host subprocess, JS/TS, stale fixtures, or in-process execution blocks promotion.
criteria.requirement: Keep normal local development available while making required runtime checks fail loudly when container evidence is requested.

failureTaxonomy.publicBehavior: Public runtime code only; no source excerpt, stream content, or stack trace.
Player-caused runtime violation when caught by validator or harness.
Existing timeout code with no private diagnostics.

failureTaxonomy.failure: Container image missing or Docker/runtime unavailable
Required cgroup or container resource feature unsupported
failureTaxonomy.publicBehavior: Required checks fail before counted runtime starts.

failureTaxonomy.failure: OOM kill or host pressure termination
failureTaxonomy.publicBehavior: Must be explicitly attributed before it can count against a Strategy.

failureTaxonomy.failure: Malformed IPC, adapter crash, or unclassified subprocess exit
failureTaxonomy.publicBehavior: Degraded/non-counted without private stream or host detail.

redactedDiagnostics.publicDiagnosticRule: Runtime readiness diagnostics expose only stable ids, public codes, readiness, limit categories, and redacted failure summaries.
